claim: Conditional workflow rules should be declarative data evaluated by a host, not executable model-authored code.
evidence: JsonLogic represents one deterministic operation per JSON rule, has no eval, setters, loops, functions, or side effects, and reads input without writing it.
notes: The page is undated; retrieval date is used. Custom operations are deliberately excluded from this factory.

claim: Conditional workflow rules should be declarative data evaluated by a host, not executable model-authored code.
evidence: Amazon States Language represents Choice rules as JSON objects with boolean/data-test expressions and explicit Next states.

claim: A conditional source needs an explicit no-match route and deterministic match policy.
evidence: ASL scans Choice rules in order, takes the first match, supports Default, and raises States.NoChoiceMatched when no rule matches and Default is absent.
notes: This factory uses explicit priority and tie failure instead of incidental array order.

claim: A conditional source needs an explicit no-match route and deterministic match policy.
evidence: Open Workflow DSL Switch supports conditional cases and permits exactly one default case without a when expression.

claim: Loops and retries must have explicit finite budgets and observable exhaustion behavior.
evidence: ASL Retry defines MaxAttempts and moves to error handling after attempts are exhausted.

claim: Loops and retries must have explicit finite budgets and observable exhaustion behavior.
evidence: Open Workflow DSL retry policies include attempt-count and duration limits with delay/backoff controls.

claim: Replayable conditional routing requires an append-only decision history bound to exact inputs and workflow definition.
evidence: Temporal documents Event History as a complete ordered log used to replay workflow code back to the exact prior state; external interactions are kept in Activities and their recorded results are reused.
notes: The docs page is continuously updated; retrieval date is used.

claim: Replayable conditional routing requires an append-only decision history bound to exact inputs and workflow definition.
evidence: Pi session files are append-only JSONL trees with stable ids and parent links, but the factory keeps workflow checkpoints external because Pi sessions and compaction are agent-context mechanisms.

claim: Pi JSON mode fits isolated bounded stages, while RPC or SDK should be opt-in for long-lived steering, cancellation, or session control.
evidence: Pi JSON mode streams session events as JSON lines for integration into other tools and custom interfaces.

claim: Pi JSON mode fits isolated bounded stages, while RPC or SDK should be opt-in for long-lived steering, cancellation, or session control.
evidence: Pinned Pi RPC docs define a long-lived stdin/stdout JSONL controller with prompt, steer, follow-up, abort, state, retry, compaction, and session commands; prompt acceptance is not completion.
notes: Same upstream project as pi.dev, but source and rendered docs were both checked.

claim: Pi stage success must be established from events and structured artifacts, not subprocess exit code alone.
evidence: Pinned print-mode source applies assistant error or aborted exit status handling only to text mode, so JSON mode can return normally while the final assistant message reports an error.

claim: Pi stage success must be established from events and structured artifacts, not subprocess exit code alone.
evidence: Anthropic recommends grading agent outcomes using deterministic final-state checks where possible rather than trusting the agent's claimed success or rigid trajectory alone.

claim: Pi discovery-disable flags do not fully isolate global model and retry configuration, so deterministic stages need a sanitized agent directory or in-memory SDK configuration.
evidence: Pi settings docs state that global settings apply across projects and configure automatic compaction and retries; project settings override them.

claim: Pi discovery-disable flags do not fully isolate global model and retry configuration, so deterministic stages need a sanitized agent directory or in-memory SDK configuration.
evidence: Pinned model docs allow models.json to override built-in provider base URLs and model definitions, while SDK docs expose in-memory SettingsManager and explicit model/resource construction.
notes: Same upstream lineage as pi.dev; verified against pinned source.
